title: Enforce authorization server-side
description: Every access decision is made on the server from the authenticated principal, never from a client-supplied role, id, or hidden field.
body: Use when adding or changing any endpoint, mutation, or resource fetch that should be restricted. Do not use client-visible flags, request-body role fields, or UI gating as the enforcement point — those are presentation only. Actions: locate the server authorization check on the path; assert the decision uses the session/token principal and the resource owner, not request input; add the check at the resource boundary, not only the route. Validation signals include a negative test that calls the endpoint as a non-owner/low-privilege principal and asserts 403/empty. Risk boundary: if no server-side check exists, treat it as a vulnerability and escalate, do not just add UI gating.
